Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes aryl migration of flavanones to form 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediates in isoflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines, downregulated in gmmyb4 mutants, and upregulated in GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; overexpression in soybean; metabolite profiling; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIFS2 expression was significantly induced by GmMYB4 and reduced in gmmyb4 mutants. 2. GmIFS2 overexpression increased total isoflavone content in leaves and seeds. 3. The GmMYB115-GmTT8 MBW complex binds the GmIFS2 promoter and represses its reporter activity. 4. GmMYB4 alleviates MBW-mediated repression of GmIFS2, promoting isoflavone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: R2-stage leaves; R8-stage seeds; soybean hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competes with anthocyanin branch; activation is associated with reduced anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for increasing seed and leaf isoflavone content; useful for stacking with upstream positive regulators such as GmMYB4 or GmMAPK6
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the mechanism, GmIFS2 is a downstream target rather than the main discovery gene.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB; contains bHLH TF-binding motif in R3 domain and EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds, with highest expression in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with bHLH proteins and interferes with formation of the GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8; GmCHR1; GmCHI1; GmIFS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes including GmIFS2 and represses anthocyanin-associated GmANS3 indirectly by disrupting MBW complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis; flavonoid/proanthocyanidin biosynthesis; branch balance between isoflavone and anthocyanin pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation through protein-protein interaction-mediated disruption of MBW complex; indirect relief of repression on GmIFS2 and reduction of activation on GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator identified by GWAS and functional characterization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; hairy root overexpression and RNAi; stable overexpression and CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; EMSA; LCI; BiFC; Y2H; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and haplotype analysis identified GmMYB4 as the candidate gene at qGlc11 associated with glycitein class content. 2. Overexpression increased total and class-specific isoflavones in hairy roots, leaves and seeds, whereas RNAi and knockout reduced them. 3. GmMYB4 interacts with bHLHs (GmTT8/GmGL3/GmEGL3) and competes with GmMYB115 for GmTT8, disrupting the MBW complex. 4. This disruption relieves repression of GmIFS2 and reduces activation of GmANS3, shifting metabolic flux toward isoflavones and away from anthocyanins. 5. GmMYB4 also promotes flavonoid and proanthocyanidin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Soybean bioactive compounds with benefits to human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates isoflavone accumulation and suppresses anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field environments for GWAS (Nanjing 2014, 2015, 2018); controlled growth chamber; R2 leaves; R8 seeds
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing for GWAS; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Anthocyanin content decreases in overexpression lines; pathway rebalancing may alter flavonoid branch outputs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and targeted overexpression/editing are promising for breeding high-isoflavone soybean varieties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A homologous gene Glyma.12g032200 shows functional compensation; double knockout caused stronger reductions, especially in glycitein class content
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S for localization/transient assays; pBA002 overexpression construct; native haplotypes analyzed

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts upstream of GmMYB4 in the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes and downregulates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis via transcriptional activation and post-translational phosphorylation of GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein phosphorylation and positive transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream positive regulator linking MAPK signaling to isoflavone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PPI prediction; BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active kinase; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification; hairy root overexpression; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Constitutively active GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 in vitro. 3. GmMAPK6 overexpression in hairy roots increases isoflavone content. 4. GmMAPK6 upregulates GmMYB4 and several key isoflavone biosynthetic genes while repressing GmANS3, supporting an upstream positive role in branch regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: STRING prediction; LC-MS/MS phosphoproteomics-like site identification; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely alters anthocyanin branch gene expression through GmANS3 downregulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upstream engineering target to elevate isoflavones through both signaling and transcriptional control; useful in stacking strategies with GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo phosphorylation was proposed but not yet fully validated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Overexpression vector in hairy roots; constitutively active mutant used for kinase assays

Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmTTG1; GmMYB4 (competitive interference via shared bHLH partner)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANS3; GmOMT5; Gm4CL; GmIFS2; GmANR2; GmF3H1; GmUGT; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: With GmTT8, binds promoters of multiple phenylpropanoid, isoflavone and anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; represses GmIFS2 reporter and activates GmANS3 reporter in MBW context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Branch regulation between isoflavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Branch-switch regulator within the MBW complex targeted by GmMYB4-mediated disruption
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB115 forms a functional MBW complex with GmTT8 and GmTTG1. 2. GmMYB4 competes with GmMYB115 for GmTT8 binding and disrupts this complex. 3. The MBW complex directly regulates multiple pathway gene promoters and suppresses GmIFS2 while activating GmANS3 reporter expression.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to balancing isoflavone versus anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As MBW component, shifts regulation toward anthocyanin-associated output and away from isoflavone-associated GmIFS2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotion of anthocyanin branch can antagonize isoflavone accumulation due to shared precursors
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to relieve repression of isoflavone biosynthesis, but likely with broader flavonoid-branch effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is mechanistically central in Results, though not the primary GWAS-discovered gene
